popularStory.description: lizabeth was born in England about 1625/6. Her father was John Moore, her mother was named Elizabeth but her last name is unknown. Her mother must have died before 1633 since her father remarried that …

zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore於1628年1月10日出生在英國艾可。她於1643年1月1日與Henry Rice在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1705年8月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆享年77歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore于1628年1月10日出生在英国艾可。她于1643年1月1日与Henry Rice在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1705年8月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆享年77岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可福拉米加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore was born on 10 January 1628, in Essex, England. She married Henry Rice on 1 January 1643, in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 23 August 1705, in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 77, and was buried in Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore est née le 10 janvier 1628, à Essex, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Henry Rice le 1 janvier 1643, à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 août 1705, à Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore è nata il il 10 gennaio 1628 nel Essex, England. Lei sposato Henry Rice il 1 gennaio 1643, nel Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 23 agosto 1705, nel Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolta a Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mooreは1628年1月10日にイギリスエセックスで生まれた。1643年1月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックススドブリーでHenry Riceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1705年8月23日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスフラミンガムで77歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスフラミンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.Sudbury, Middlesex County, Massachusetts: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이
ko.Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Co: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이
ko.Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 프램잉함

ko.Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Ba: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 말를버루
ko.Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore(은)는 1628년 1월 10일 영국 에세스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1643년 1월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 Henry Rice(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1705년 8월 23일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 프램잉함에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 프램잉함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore nasceu em 10 de janeiro de 1628, em Essex, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Henry Rice em 1 de janeiro de 1643, em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de agosto de 1705, em Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore родилась 10 января 1628 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Rice 1 января 1643 г. в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 23 августа 1705 г. в местности фрамингхам, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности фрамингхам, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Moore nació el 10 de enero de 1628, en Essex, Inglaterra. Se casó con Henry Rice el 1 de enero de 1643, en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 23 de agosto de 1705, en Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Framingham, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
